paper.title: A binary system in the S cluster close to the supermassive black hole Sagittarius A*

extraction.summary: Paper reports the detection of a spectroscopic binary system D9 in the S cluster near Sgr A*. The system is explicitly described as bound to Sgr A* on a stable, mildly eccentric orbit. The paper does not present any objects as possibly unbound from the Milky Way or as Galactic-escape candidates. All tables contain bound binary parameters, radial-velocity epochs, and observation logs for the D9 system. No HVS/unbound candidates are identified.

candidate_groups_considered.description: The D9 binary system (primary D9a and secondary D9b) orbiting Sgr A*.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The paper explicitly describes D9 as bound to Sgr A* on a stable Keplerian orbit with semi-major axis 44 mpc and eccentricity 0.32. It states the system 'remains in a stable, mildly eccentric orbit around Sgr A*' and 'qualifies as an S cluster member.' This is a Galactic-center-bound binary, not a candidate for Galactic unbinding.

source_refs.context: Abstract states D9 is a binary system in the S cluster close to Sgr A* with stable orbit against disruption.
Paper states the system remains in a stable, mildly eccentric orbit around Sgr A* and can be described as a hard binary.

candidate_groups_considered.description: Overview table of known binaries in the Galactic center.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: This table lists confirmed binaries in the Galactic center (including D9 as the only S-cluster member). These are all bound systems near Sgr A*; the paper does not present any of them as possibly unbound from the Galaxy.

source_refs.context: Table caption and content list binaries of the Galactic center with D9 as the only S cluster member.

candidate_groups_considered.description: S-cluster stars (high-velocity stars and G-objects) mentioned in the abstract and introduction.
candidate_groups_considered.reason: The abstract mentions 'high-velocity stars and peculiar G objects orbit the central supermassive black hole' as constituents of the S cluster. The introduction clarifies these stars orbit Sgr A* 'at velocities of up to several thousand km/s' inside the S cluster. These are bound to the SMBH, not escaping the Galaxy. The paper does not claim any S-cluster star is unbound from the Milky Way.

source_refs.context: Abstract mentions high-velocity stars and G objects orbit Sgr A* as the S cluster.
Introduction states stars in the S cluster orbit the black hole at high velocities inside the S cluster.
